title: calylophus hartwegii subsp. pubescens
intro: Calylophus hartwegii subsp. pubescens, also known as the pubescent Mexican primrose, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Calylophus hartwegii subsp. pubescens include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and it can be applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
